reason: Geometric median via Weiszfeld iteration scales super-linearly with gradient dimension at the scales we measured. Scalability anchor measured 9.03 s/round at 10M parameters for GeoMed; a naive 12.4x extrapolation to 124M is ~112 s/round of aggregation alone, dominating the ~50 s network + compute round time for every other defense. Running 200 rounds would take >= 6 hours of pure aggregation time on top of compute and network, which is both economically wasteful on Akash and methodologically uninformative (it tests the aggregator implementation's scaling, not Byzantine resilience).

evidence.extrapolation_rule: linear in D (actual scaling is worse than linear at high D for Weiszfeld); this is a lower bound on cost

recommendation: Exclude GeoMed from the Phase 2 Akash run matrix. Keep the scalability measurement as the evidence in the paper (Section 6 per agent_docs/paper_structure.md). If GeoMed must be tested, do so only on the tiny model variant.
